# quotedprintable

```go
import "mime/quotedprintable"
```

Package quotedprintable implements quoted\-printable encoding as specified by RFC 2045.

## Index

- [type Reader](<#Reader>)
  - [func NewReader\(r io.Reader\) \*Reader](<#NewReader>)
  - [func \(r \*Reader\) Read\(p \[\]byte\) \(n int, err error\)](<#Reader.Read>)

<a name="Reader"></a>

## type [Reader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/quotedprintable/reader.go#L17-L21>)

Reader is a quoted\-printable decoder.

```go
type Reader struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewReader"></a>

### func [NewReader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/quotedprintable/reader.go#L24>)

```go
func NewReader(r io.Reader) *Reader
```

NewReader returns a quoted\-printable reader, decoding from r.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"mime/quotedprintable"
	"strings"
)

func main() {
	for _, s := range []string{
		`=48=65=6C=6C=6F=2C=20=47=6F=70=68=65=72=73=21`,
		`invalid escape: <b style="font-size: 200%">hello</b>`,
		"Hello, Gophers! This symbol will be unescaped: =3D and this will be written in =\r\none line.",
	} {
		b, err := io.ReadAll(quotedprintable.NewReader(strings.NewReader(s)))
		fmt.Printf("%s %v\n", b, err)
	}
}
```

#### Output

```go
Hello, Gophers! <nil>
invalid escape: <b style="font-size: 200%">hello</b> <nil>
Hello, Gophers! This symbol will be unescaped: = and this will be written in one line. <nil>
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Reader.Read"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [Read](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/quotedprintable/reader.go#L73>)

```go
func (r *Reader) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Read reads and decodes quoted\-printable data from the underlying reader.

<a name="Writer"></a>

## type [Writer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/quotedprintable/writer.go#L12-L21>)

A Writer is a quoted\-printable writer that implements [io.WriteCloser](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#WriteCloser>).

```go
type Writer struct {
    // Binary mode treats the writer's input as pure binary and processes end of
    // line bytes as binary data.
    Binary bool
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewWriter"></a>

### func [NewWriter](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/quotedprintable/writer.go#L24>)

```go
func NewWriter(w io.Writer) *Writer
```

NewWriter returns a new [Writer](<#Writer>) that writes to w.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"mime/quotedprintable"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	w := quotedprintable.NewWriter(os.Stdout)
	w.Write([]byte("These symbols will be escaped: = \t"))
	w.Close()

}
```

#### Output

```go
These symbols will be escaped: =3D =09
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Writer.Close"></a>

### func \(\*Writer\) [Close](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/quotedprintable/writer.go#L67>)

```go
func (w *Writer) Close() error
```

Close closes the [Writer](<#Writer>), flushing any unwritten data to the underlying [io.Writer](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#Writer>), but does not close the underlying io.Writer.

<a name="Writer.Write"></a>

### func \(\*Writer\) [Write](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/quotedprintable/writer.go#L31>)

```go
func (w *Writer) Write(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Write encodes p using quoted\-printable encoding and writes it to the underlying [io.Writer](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#Writer>). It limits line length to 76 characters. The encoded bytes are not necessarily flushed until the [Writer](<#Writer>) is closed.
